channel.title: NYT > World > Asia Pacific
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Visit to a Temple at the Heart of the Thailand-Cambodia Conflict
item.description: A rare visit to a Khmer temple on Thailand and Cambodia’s border showed how deadly clashes between the two countries have scarred a heritage site.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Lauren DeCicca
item.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times

item.title: How the War in Iran Could Help China and Change Asia
item.description: American officials have said for years that they would prioritize the Indo-Pacific. Now they’re moving warships, missiles and air defenses out for a war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave, Choe Sang-Hun, Javier C. Hernández and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Fighter jets on the aircraft carrier U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln in 2024. A carrier group including the ship was redirected from the South China Sea to the Middle East in January.

item.title: Trump Targets Forced Labor in Global Tariff Scheme
item.description: The Trump administration began a trade investigation Thursday into whether dozens of countries have policies to combat forced labor.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: Office of the United States Trade Representative
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Trucks leaving the Port of Los Angeles last May. The latest trade investigation is part of an effort by the Trump administration to resurrect a global system of tariffs struck down by the Supreme Court last month.

item.title: Iran’s Frantic Attempt to Save Its Ships Before Torpedo Attack
item.description: The Iranian Navy sought refuge in Sri Lanka and India. While India obliged, Sri Lanka stalled over fears it would threaten its neutrality.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Pamodi Waravita
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Iranian destroyer Dena took part in a fleet review near Visakhapatnam, India, last month, just days before a United States submarine sank the warship with a torpedo.

item.title: China Wants Its Ethnic Minorities to Blend In. Now It’s the Law.
item.description: Under a new “ethnic unity” law, Mandarin Chinese must now be the language of teaching. Parents must guide their children to love the Communist Party. Neighborhoods should be mixed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.media:description: Ethnic minority delegates arriving for a meeting of the National People’s Congress, the rubber-stamp legislature, in Beijing last week.

item.title: San Francisco’s Chinatown Celebrated Eileen Gu. Others Are More Conflicted.
item.description: Conservatives have rebuked Ms. Gu, who was born in the United States but won Olympic medals for China. The reaction has sparked conversations among Chinese Americans about identity and straddling two worlds.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin and Coral Murphy Marcos
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ms. Gu has often said that “When I’m in the U.S., I’m American, but when I’m in China, I’m Chinese.”

item.title: At China’s Big Political Meeting, a Rare Debate About Inequality
item.description: China’s plan to raise pensions for farmers by less than $3 a month prompted rare criticism from lawmakers about the country’s threadbare social safety net.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Andrea Verdelli for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Chinese government said it would increase the minimum basic pension for rural residents by 20 yuan a month, or about $3, to a grand total of 163 yuan a month, or about $24.

item.title: Strong or Weak? How Trump Picks His Battles.
item.description: Despite his tough talk, President Trump has consistently made allowances for countries he sees as powerful or dominant.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia during a meeting in Alaska last summer. Mr. Trump’s deference to Russia highlights a pattern that has crystallized in recent months as he launches military action overseas.

item.title: In Japan, Making Wooden Kokeshi Dolls
item.description: A Japanese artisan demonstrates the skill needed to produce kokeshi.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Morelli and Andrew Faulk
item.media:description: The family of Teruyuki Hiraga has been making kokeshi, or traditional Japanese wooden dolls, for eight generations.

item.title: World Leaders Will Release 400 Million Barrels of Oil to Stabilize Prices
item.description: The members of the International Energy Agency will release 400 million barrels of oil, the largest such coordinated action on record.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Trump-Xi Summit Nears, but China Doesn’t Know What Trump Wants
item.description: Trump’s agenda for the high-stakes meeting remains unclear to Beijing, Chinese analysts say, while American executives say they haven’t been invited along.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and David Pierson
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, meeting in Busan, South Korea, in October, where they announced a yearlong trade truce.

item.title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan
item.description: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: A photo released last year by Chinese state media showing aircraft on the Fujian, China’s third aircraft carrier.

item.title: One Member of Iranian Soccer Squad Abandons Asylum Bid in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the Iranian women’s soccer delegation had initially opted to stay in Australia. The reversal was a sign of how fraught that decision was.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s soccer team arriving in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, after flying from Australia.

item.title: Cost-Cutting Led to South Korean Airport’s Deadly Wall, Report Finds
item.description: The concrete runway barrier played a key role in a disaster that killed 179 people. An audit revealed officials skimped on construction fees and then falsified records.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Jeju Air Flight 2216 crashed into a concrete barrier at the end of the runway at Muan International Airport, South Korea, in late 2024.

item.title: Pakistan Sends Navy Escort for Its Ships in the Middle East
item.description: Pakistan, Iran’s neighbor, has been striving to remain neutral in the conflict. But its government and military have been forced to act to protect its economy.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pakistan’s government has announced stringent fuel-saving measures as the conflict in the Middle East chokes oil supplies from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: China Stockpiled More Oil Before War Broke Out in Iran
item.description: China’s oil imports rose nearly 16 percent in the first two months of 2026, adding to its growing energy stockpile.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil tanker unloading crude oil at a terminal at the port in Qingdao, in China’s eastern Shandong province, on Tuesday

item.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, the Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
item.description: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire and Sajal Pradhan
item.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
item.media:description: Balendra Shah campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: Australia Grants Humanitarian Visas to 5 Members of Iranian Women’s Soccer Team
item.description: Concern for the safety of the players had grown after Iranian state media criticized them for not singing the national anthem at a game in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim and Laura Chung
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iranian national women’s soccer team played the Philippines in its last match in the Asian Cup tournament in Australia on Sunday.

item.title: G7 Countries Hold Off on Releasing Emergency Oil Supplies
item.description: Finance ministers for the seven industrialized countries met on Monday and said they would consider releasing oil from reserves but were not ready to do so now.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Smoke rose from a refinery operated by Bapco Energies, Bahrain’s state-owned energy company, on Monday. Global oil prices have spiked since the U.S.-led war with Iran began.

item.title: Countries in Asia Try to Contain the Economic Fallout of the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Across Asia, where countries are highly exposed to rising oil and gas costs and tightening supply, governments are acting to mitigate economic harm.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik, Choe Sang-Hun, Sui-Lee Wee, Meaghan Tobin and Damien Cave
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow passage between Iran and Oman. Historically 20 million barrels pass through it each day, most of it bound for Asia.
